response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and web engineer with broad experience, but he does not meet the core requirements of this role. His career centers on TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang for web platforms, with only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments a decade ago. Critically, he lists no Japanese language ability at all, where native-level proficiency is a hard requirement, and shows no evidence of Linux kernel modification, L2/L3 switch development, or security technologies such as MACsec or 802.1X. He is also based in Berlin with no indication of relocation intent.
strengths: Long, varied engineering career (10+ years) with strong software craftsmanship practices such as TDD and CI/CD
Some low-level and systems exposure: C/C++ at National Instruments, low-latency DLang services, Linux experience, and hardware work with robotic arms
Fluent English with a track record of open-source contributions, workshops, and knowledge sharing
concerns: No Japanese proficiency listed, failing a non-negotiable native-level Japanese requirement
No Linux kernel, embedded networking, switch, or security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X, SNMP/NETCONF) experience; C work is minimal and dated
Career trajectory is web/full-stack and product-focused, a poor match for embedded firmware development on LAN-switch products

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or kernel experience
Solid general engineering and some systems-level background
